evidence.name: six_workload_physical_compact
evidence.scope: public DOGI prototype on physical ZNS over six DOGI-shaped compact traces

evidence.name: dynamic_pressure_dogi
evidence.scope: public DOGI prototype on Alibaba-like p8000 compact pressure trace

evidence.name: dynamic_pressure_selector_suite
evidence.scope: public DOGI prototype DOGI/Greedy/CostBenefit selector variants

evidence.name: original_lba_dynamic_dogi
evidence.scope: public DOGI prototype on original-LBA Alibaba-like p8000 span

paper_rule: Use same-path baselines for apples-to-apples QUASAR/FIFO/SepBIT/MiDAS/DOGI-style replay. Use exact public DOGI runs only as sanity evidence and never unit-mix their internal GiB counters with packed-ZNS replay WAF or latency.
remaining_parity_gaps: QUASAR is not implemented inside the public DOGI/ZenFS stack.
The exact public DOGI counters and QUASAR packed-replay counters use different units and stack boundaries.
Compact-LBA DOGI runs are feasibility evidence; the original-LBA run exists for one hard dynamic trace only.
A full production comparison would need the same app/ZenFS/SPDK path for DOGI and QUASAR.
reviewer_answer: The headline same-path DOGI-style baseline is not the public DOGI prototype. The paper therefore keeps exact public DOGI evidence separate: null_blk/ZenFS, six compact physical-ZNS workloads, a dynamic p8000 pressure run, selector variants, and an original-LBA p8000 run all complete. This is substantial direct evidence, but not full end-to-end parity with QUASAR's packed replay.
